title: Adelocaryum malabaricum
taxonomy: Adelocaryum malabaricum is classified under the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. This family of flowering plants is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Adelocaryum malabaricum falls under the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants like mint and oregano. The plant's classification is further grouped under the subclass Asteridae, which comprises various flowering plant families. The classification of Adelocaryum malabaricum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
uses: Adelocaryum malabaricum is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Its roots and leaves are used to prepare medicinal concoctions and infusions. Adelocaryum malabaricum is also believed to have hepatoprotective and gastroprotective effects, making it a potential treatment for liver and stomach ailments. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Adelocaryum malabaricum for medicinal purposes.
